opportunities.title: Raleigh Parks Summer Day Camp Counselor
opportunities.short_summary: A paid part-time summer position for teens ages 16 and up to work as day camp counselors with Raleigh Parks and Recreation.
opportunities.full_description: Raleigh Parks hires seasonal part-time staff each summer, including day camp counselor positions for teens ages 16 and older. Counselors help lead activities, supervise campers, and ensure a safe and fun environment at city recreation centers and park sites. The role typically requires 35 to 50 hours per week during June through August and pays approximately $12 per hour.
opportunities.eligibility: Must be at least 16 years old.
opportunities.compensation_text: Approximately $12 per hour
opportunities.time_commitment: 35 to 50 hours per week, June through August
opportunities.source_notes: Positions are posted on the City of Raleigh jobs portal; hiring is ongoing until filled.

opportunities.title: Raleigh Counselor in Training (CIT) Program
opportunities.short_summary: A training program for teens ages 14 to 16 to learn camp counselor skills and then volunteer with Raleigh Parks summer camps.
opportunities.full_description: The Counselor in Training (CIT) Program through Raleigh Parks and Recreation gives teens ages 14 to 16 a structured introduction to camp leadership. Participants attend a training session covering safety, communication, and activity planning, then volunteer alongside experienced counselors at city summer camps. The program helps teens build leadership skills and prepare for future paid counselor positions. There is a $250 program fee.
opportunities.eligibility: Must be 14 to 16 years old.
opportunities.cost_text: $250 program fee
opportunities.time_commitment: Training June 15 to 18, then volunteer shifts through the summer
opportunities.source_notes: Details based on the 2025 program. Check the City of Raleigh website for updated 2026 dates and registration.

opportunities.title: Wake County Youth Health Council
opportunities.short_summary: A volunteer leadership group for rising 9th through 12th graders focused on public health initiatives in Wake County.
opportunities.full_description: The Wake County Youth Health Council brings together high school students who are interested in public health and community wellness. Members meet bi-monthly to plan and carry out health-focused initiatives, participate in service projects, and develop leadership skills. The council is organized by Wake County Public Health and registration for each new school year cycle typically opens in July.
opportunities.eligibility: Must be a rising 9th through 12th grader in Wake County.
opportunities.time_commitment: Bi-monthly meetings during the school year
opportunities.source_notes: 2026-2027 registration expected to open in July 2026.

opportunities.title: Wake County Teen Spring Kickback
opportunities.short_summary: A free one-day health and empowerment event for Wake County high school students at Urban Air Raleigh on April 6, 2026.
opportunities.full_description: The Wake County Teen Spring Kickback is a free event open to high school students. It takes place on April 6, 2026, from 8:30am to 5pm at Urban Air Raleigh. The event focuses on health, wellness, and empowerment through interactive workshops, guest speakers, activities, and networking. Registration is required and space may be limited.
opportunities.eligibility: Open to high school students in Wake County. Free to attend.
opportunities.time_commitment: One day, April 6, 2026, 8:30am to 5pm

opportunities.title: NC State Young Investigators Nuclear Engineering Program
opportunities.short_summary: A three-week residential summer program at NC State for rising juniors, seniors, and graduating seniors to explore nuclear engineering.
opportunities.full_description: The Young Investigators Program in Nuclear Engineering at NC State University offers a three-week residential experience from July 6 to 24, 2026. Students explore nuclear science and engineering through hands-on labs, lectures, and research activities. The program is all-inclusive at a cost of $2,400 covering housing, meals, and materials. Applications are due by April 30, 2026.
opportunities.eligibility: Open to rising high school juniors, seniors, and graduating seniors.
opportunities.cost_text: $2,400 all-inclusive (housing, meals, materials)
opportunities.time_commitment: 3 weeks residential, July 6 to 24, 2026

opportunities.title: NC State Horticultural Science Summer Institute
opportunities.short_summary: A one-week residential program at NC State for rising 9th through 12th graders to explore plant science, sustainability, and horticulture careers.
opportunities.full_description: The Horticultural Science Summer Institute at NC State University is a week-long residential program running July 5 to 10, 2026. Students participate in hands-on activities related to plant science, landscape design, food production, and sustainability. The cost is $675, which includes lodging, meals, and all program materials. Scholarships are available for students with financial need. The application deadline is April 1, 2026.
opportunities.eligibility: Open to rising 9th through 12th graders.
opportunities.cost_text: $675 (includes lodging and meals). Scholarships available.
opportunities.time_commitment: 1 week residential, July 5 to 10, 2026
opportunities.source_notes: Application deadline is April 1, 2026, which is 2 days away.

opportunities.title: NC State Summer Textile Exploration Program (STEP)
opportunities.short_summary: A one-week summer program at NC State's Wilson College of Textiles where high school students explore textile science, fashion, and design.
opportunities.full_description: The Summer Textile Exploration Program (STEP) at NC State's Wilson College of Textiles offers high school students a week of hands-on experience in textile engineering, fashion design, and polymer science. Multiple sessions are available during June and July 2026. The cost is $450 per session, and scholarships are available for qualifying students.
opportunities.eligibility: Open to high school students.
opportunities.cost_text: $450 per session. Scholarships available.
opportunities.time_commitment: 1-week sessions in June and July 2026

opportunities.title: NC State Engineering Residential Program
opportunities.short_summary: A one-week residential summer program at NC State for rising 11th and 12th graders to explore engineering disciplines through labs and projects.
opportunities.full_description: The High School Engineering Residential Program at NC State offers rising juniors and seniors a week-long immersive experience in various engineering fields. Students live on campus, participate in hands-on labs, hear from faculty, and work on team projects. Multiple sessions are typically offered in June and July. Applications for the 2026 season have closed.
opportunities.eligibility: Open to rising 11th and 12th graders.
opportunities.cost_text: Varies by session
opportunities.time_commitment: 1-week sessions, June to July 2026
opportunities.source_notes: Applications for 2026 have closed.

opportunities.title: Duke Pre-College Summer Program
opportunities.short_summary: An enrichment program through Duke TIP offering 12-day academic sessions for motivated students in grades 6 through 12.
opportunities.full_description: The Duke TIP Pre-College Summer Program provides academically talented students in grades 6 through 12 with intensive, college-level coursework in a variety of subjects. Three 12-day sessions are offered: June 13 to 24, June 28 to July 9, and July 13 to 24, 2026. Commuter tuition is approximately $3,950 per session. Students choose from courses in humanities, sciences, social sciences, and more, taught by experienced instructors on the Duke campus.
opportunities.eligibility: Students in grades 6 through 12. Admission is selective.
opportunities.cost_text: Approximately $3,950 commuter tuition per session
opportunities.time_commitment: 12-day sessions: June 13-24, June 28-July 9, July 13-24
opportunities.source_notes: Duke is in Durham County, but the program is widely attended by Wake County students.

opportunities.title: Food Bank of Central & Eastern NC Volunteer
opportunities.short_summary: Sort and pack food at the Raleigh distribution center. Teens 16 and older can volunteer independently; younger teens must be accompanied by an adult.
opportunities.full_description: The Food Bank of Central & Eastern NC welcomes volunteers at its Raleigh distribution center to help sort, inspect, and pack donated food for distribution to families in need. Volunteers under 16 must be accompanied by a parent or guardian. Teens 16 and older may volunteer independently. Shifts are available on a flexible schedule, and groups are welcome. No prior experience is needed.
opportunities.eligibility: Under 16 must be accompanied by an adult. Ages 16 and older can volunteer independently.
opportunities.source_name: Food Bank of Central & Eastern NC
opportunities.time_commitment: Flexible scheduling, various shift times available

opportunities.title: Habitat for Humanity Wake County Summer of Service
opportunities.short_summary: A structured summer volunteer program for teens ages 16 to 19 to gain hands-on construction experience building homes in Wake County.
opportunities.full_description: Habitat for Humanity of Wake County's Summer of Service program engages teens ages 16 to 19 in hands-on homebuilding and community development. Participants work on active construction sites alongside experienced supervisors three days per week during the summer. No prior construction experience is required. The program teaches practical skills while making a direct impact on affordable housing.
opportunities.eligibility: Must be 16 to 19 years old.
opportunities.source_name: Habitat for Humanity of Wake County
opportunities.time_commitment: 3 days per week during summer

opportunities.title: Habitat for Humanity Wake County Construction Volunteer
opportunities.short_summary: Year-round construction site volunteering for teens ages 16 and up, helping build affordable homes in Wake County.
opportunities.full_description: Habitat for Humanity of Wake County welcomes volunteers ages 16 and older to help on construction sites throughout the year. Tasks may include framing, painting, landscaping, and other building activities under the guidance of trained site supervisors. No experience is necessary. This is a great way for teens to earn community service hours while contributing to affordable housing.
opportunities.eligibility: Must be at least 16 years old.
opportunities.source_name: Habitat for Humanity of Wake County
opportunities.time_commitment: Flexible, year-round

opportunities.title: WakeMed Junior Volunteer Program
opportunities.short_summary: A hospital volunteer program for high school students ages 16 and older with a six-month minimum commitment at WakeMed facilities.
opportunities.full_description: The WakeMed Junior Volunteer Program allows high school students ages 16 and older to gain firsthand experience in a healthcare setting. Junior volunteers assist with patient comfort, wayfinding, clerical support, and other tasks across WakeMed facilities. A minimum six-month commitment is required. Applications open periodically throughout the year, and accepted volunteers receive orientation and training.
opportunities.eligibility: Must be at least 16 years old and a current high school student.
opportunities.time_commitment: Minimum 6-month commitment, regular weekly shifts
opportunities.source_notes: Applications open periodically. Check the WakeMed website for current availability.

opportunities.title: NC Museum of Natural Sciences Cafe Coordinators
opportunities.short_summary: A teen leadership program where grades 9 through 12 students plan and run monthly Teen Science Cafe events at the NC Museum of Natural Sciences.
opportunities.full_description: The Cafe Coordinators program at the NC Museum of Natural Sciences recruits high school students in grades 9 through 12 to plan and facilitate monthly Teen Science Cafe events. Coordinators meet bi-monthly on Wednesdays from 4 to 6pm, September through April, and organize events that connect teens with working scientists. There is a $75 annual fee, and scholarships are available. The application deadline is April 20, 2026.
opportunities.eligibility: Students in grades 9 through 12.
opportunities.cost_text: $75 annual fee. Scholarships available.
opportunities.time_commitment: Bi-monthly meetings Wed 4-6pm, September to April

opportunities.title: NC Museum of Art Teen Arts Council
opportunities.short_summary: A leadership council for teens ages 13 to 18 who meet weekly at the NC Museum of Art to plan events, workshops, and creative programming.
opportunities.full_description: The Teen Arts Council at the NC Museum of Art brings together teens ages 13 to 18 to help shape the museum's teen programming. Members meet Thursdays from 4:30 to 6:30pm during the school year (September through June) and work together to plan teen events, connect with visiting artists, and develop workshops. Applications for the 2026-2027 cohort are expected to open in fall 2026.
opportunities.eligibility: Ages 13 to 18.
opportunities.time_commitment: Thursdays 4:30 to 6:30pm, September through June
opportunities.source_notes: Applications for the current year are closed. 2026-2027 applications expected to open in fall 2026.

opportunities.title: NC Museum of Art Teens Sketch the Galleries
opportunities.short_summary: A free drop-in sketching program for teens ages 13 to 18 at the NC Museum of Art, with upcoming sessions on April 4 and May 2, 2026.
opportunities.full_description: Teens Sketch the Galleries is a free, drop-in program at the NC Museum of Art where teens ages 13 to 18 can sketch works in the museum's galleries with guidance from teaching artists. No registration is required. Upcoming dates include April 4 and May 2, 2026. Materials are provided, and participants of all skill levels are welcome.
opportunities.eligibility: Ages 13 to 18. No registration required.
opportunities.time_commitment: Drop-in, select dates (April 4, May 2, 2026)

opportunities.title: Raleigh Little Theatre Teens on Stage
opportunities.short_summary: A five-week summer acting intensive at Raleigh Little Theatre for rising 10th graders through 2026 graduates, with admission by audition.
opportunities.full_description: Teens on Stage is a selective five-week summer intensive at Raleigh Little Theatre focused on acting. The program is open to rising 10th graders through 2026 high school graduates and culminates in a full production. Participants develop skills in acting technique, scene study, voice, and movement. Admission is by audition only, and auditions for the 2026 season have been completed.
opportunities.eligibility: Rising 10th graders through 2026 high school graduates. Admission by audition.
opportunities.cost_text: Contact Raleigh Little Theatre for tuition information
opportunities.time_commitment: 5-week summer intensive
opportunities.source_notes: Auditions for 2026 have been completed.

opportunities.title: Raleigh Little Theatre Teens Backstage
opportunities.short_summary: A five-week summer intensive in technical theatre at Raleigh Little Theatre for rising 10th graders through 2026 graduates, with only 6 to 8 spots.
opportunities.full_description: Teens Backstage is a five-week summer intensive at Raleigh Little Theatre focused on technical theatre, including set construction, lighting, sound, costumes, and stage management. The program accepts only 6 to 8 students, making it highly selective. It is open to rising 10th graders through 2026 high school graduates and admission is by interview. The 2026 cohort has already been selected.
opportunities.eligibility: Rising 10th graders through 2026 high school graduates. Admission by interview.
opportunities.cost_text: Contact Raleigh Little Theatre for tuition information
opportunities.time_commitment: 5-week summer intensive
opportunities.source_notes: Only 6 to 8 spots available. 2026 selections completed.

opportunities.title: Carolina Tiger Rescue High School Volunteer
opportunities.short_summary: Volunteer at a wildlife sanctuary near Wake County in roles including gift shop, office support, and construction projects. Ages 16 and up.
opportunities.full_description: Carolina Tiger Rescue in Pittsboro, NC, offers volunteer opportunities for high school students ages 16 and older. Roles include staffing the gift shop, assisting with office and administrative tasks, and helping with construction and maintenance projects at the sanctuary. This is a great way to support animal welfare and gain community service hours. The sanctuary is located in Chatham County, a short drive from Wake County.
opportunities.eligibility: Must be at least 16 years old.
opportunities.time_commitment: Flexible, year-round
opportunities.source_notes: Located in Pittsboro, Chatham County, near the Wake County border.

opportunities.title: YMCA Southeast Raleigh Fellowship
opportunities.short_summary: A 24-week leadership and career development program for ages 16 to 24, including a paid 50-hour internship placement.
opportunities.full_description: The YMCA Southeast Raleigh Fellowship is a 24-week program running from January through June that combines leadership development, career readiness workshops, and a paid 50-hour internship. The program is free to participants ages 16 to 24 and is designed to build workforce skills and professional networks. The 2026 cohort began in January and is currently in progress.
opportunities.eligibility: Ages 16 to 24.
opportunities.compensation_text: Includes a paid 50-hour internship
opportunities.time_commitment: 24 weeks, January through June
opportunities.source_notes: 2026 cohort is currently in progress. Applications for 2027 typically open in fall.

opportunities.title: YMCA Youth & Government
opportunities.short_summary: A civics education program through the YMCA of the Triangle where high school students simulate state government proceedings.
opportunities.full_description: YMCA Youth & Government is a statewide civic engagement program for high school students. Participants learn about government by writing bills, debating legislation, running campaigns, and participating in a model legislative session at the NC General Assembly. The program runs during the school year with local club meetings and culminates in a state conference. Students join through their local YMCA branch.
opportunities.eligibility: Open to high school students.
opportunities.cost_text: Program fees vary by YMCA branch
opportunities.time_commitment: School year, weekly club meetings plus state conference

opportunities.title: YMCA Leaders Club
opportunities.short_summary: A YMCA leadership development and community service program for high school students in the Triangle area.
opportunities.full_description: The YMCA Leaders Club provides high school students with opportunities to develop leadership skills through community service projects, teamwork exercises, and personal development workshops. Members meet regularly at their local YMCA branch and participate in service activities throughout the year. The program encourages teens to take on active roles in their communities.
opportunities.eligibility: Open to high school students.
opportunities.time_commitment: Regular meetings during the school year

opportunities.title: Wake County 4-H Teen Council
opportunities.short_summary: A 4-H leadership group for Wake County teens who meet monthly to plan youth programs, community events, and service projects.
opportunities.full_description: The Wake County 4-H Teen Council is a leadership body within the local 4-H program. Teen members meet monthly to plan and lead programs for younger 4-H members, organize community service projects, and develop their own leadership abilities. Participation is open to teens involved in Wake County 4-H and provides opportunities for public speaking, event planning, and mentoring.
opportunities.eligibility: Open to teens enrolled in Wake County 4-H (ages 5 to 19 eligible for 4-H).
opportunities.time_commitment: Monthly meetings during the school year

opportunities.title: Wake County 4-H Liberation Out Loud
opportunities.short_summary: A 4-H program for Wake County high school students centered on social justice education, community organizing, and service.
opportunities.full_description: Liberation Out Loud is a Wake County 4-H program that engages high school students in exploring social justice topics, community organizing, and civic engagement. Members participate in discussions, workshops, and service projects focused on equity, inclusion, and systemic change. The program builds leadership skills while encouraging teens to take action on issues they care about.
opportunities.eligibility: Open to high school students in Wake County.
opportunities.time_commitment: Regular meetings during the school year

opportunities.title: Wake County 4-H Summer Sleepaway Camp
opportunities.short_summary: A week-long residential 4-H camp at the Betsey-Jeff Penn Center for ages 8 to 17, with teen-specific tracks for older campers.
opportunities.full_description: Wake County 4-H Summer Camp is a week-long residential experience at the Betsey-Jeff Penn Center, running June 21 to 26, 2026. The camp offers outdoor activities, STEM workshops, leadership development, and team-building for youth ages 8 to 17. Teen tracks are available for ages 12 to 14 and ages 15 to 17, including a Leaders in Training (L.I.T.) program for older teens. Cost is $605, and scholarships are available.
opportunities.eligibility: Ages 8 to 17. Teen tracks for ages 12 to 14 and 15 to 17.
opportunities.cost_text: $605. Scholarships available.
opportunities.time_commitment: 1 week residential, June 21 to 26, 2026
opportunities.source_notes: Camp held at Betsey-Jeff Penn Center. L.I.T. program available for ages 15 to 17.

A June 2025 WakeEd news article about the summer cohort also listed a $150 stipend for students who complete key program elements and referenced transportation support; stipends and logistics can change by year, so confirm with WakeEd or your school for the current cycle.

opportunities.source_notes: Primary source: WakeEd Career Accelerator hub. Stipend/transport details were quoted from WakeEd’s live June 12, 2025 news post (still online at verification); hub page updated Feb 25, 2026.

The council gives teens a voice in local government and helps build civic engagement skills. Participation requires a $35 registration fee as published on the Town’s Teen Council page.
opportunities.eligibility: Students in grades 8-12 who live or attend school in Cary, NC (confirm current year rules on the official page).
opportunities.cost_text: $35 registration fee (annual, per Town of Cary Teen Council information).
opportunities.time_commitment: Quarterly meetings plus community service events
opportunities.source_notes: Registration fee and grade eligibility confirmed against Town program materials; verify any changes on the official Cary page.

opportunities.title: Dorothea Dix Park TeenSpace Programs
opportunities.short_summary: Free programs at Dorothea Dix Park for ages 12 to 18, including yoga, art workshops, and a teen creator market.
opportunities.full_description: Dorothea Dix Park in Raleigh offers free TeenSpace programs for young people ages 12 to 18. opportunities.time_commitment: Varies by event

opportunities.title: NC Governor's School

opportunities.full_description: The Governor's School of North Carolina is a state-funded, tuition-free residential program for gifted rising juniors and seniors. The 2026 session runs June 21 to July 18. Students are nominated by their school counselors and selected through a competitive process. The program offers intensive study in areas such as mathematics, science, social science, and the arts. Selections for the 2026 cohort were made in March 2026.
opportunities.eligibility: Rising juniors and seniors in North Carolina public or private schools. Nomination by school counselor required.

opportunities.full_description: The Teen Leadership Corps at Wake County Public Libraries gives students in grades 6 through 10 the chance to volunteer at their local library branch. opportunities.full_description: Code the Dream offers a free, intensive coding bootcamp that is fully remote and open to participants from diverse backgrounds. Students choose between web development and data engineering tracks and commit to approximately 20 hours per week of coursework, projects, and mentorship. opportunities.short_summary: A one-on-one mentoring program matching youth ages 6 to 14 with caring adult volunteers in Wake, Durham, and Orange counties.
opportunities.full_description: Big Brothers Big Sisters of the Triangle provides one-on-one mentoring relationships for youth ages 6 to 14 (as Littles) paired with adult volunteers (as Bigs). opportunities.title: AMC Math Competitions
opportunities.short_summary: The American Mathematics Competitions (AMC 10 and AMC 12) for high school students, held annually in November through schools.
opportunities.full_description: The AMC 10 and AMC 12 are 25-question, 75-minute multiple-choice exams administered by the Mathematical Association of America. The AMC 10 covers math typically taught through 10th grade, while the AMC 12 covers the full high school curriculum. Top scorers qualify for the AIME (American Invitational Mathematics Examination) and potentially the USA Mathematical Olympiad. Students participate through their schools during the November testing window. The 2025-2026 competition season has concluded.
opportunities.eligibility: AMC 10: students in grade 10 or below. AMC 12: students in grade 12 or below.
opportunities.cost_text: Registration through school
opportunities.time_commitment: Annual competition in November
opportunities.source_notes: 2025-2026 competition season concluded. Register through your school for the 2026-2027 season in fall.

opportunities.full_description: The state parks volunteer program includes trail work, interpretation, special events, and habitat projects. Wake County teens often visit nearby parks such as Jordan Lake; check each park's page for local needs.

opportunities.cost_text: Fees if listed on registration.
opportunities.time_commitment: Scheduled family sessions.

opportunities.title: Wake County Public Libraries Summer Reading 2026
opportunities.short_summary: Summer reading challenge and teen reads lists from Wake County Public Libraries, including the annual bookmark contest.

opportunities.time_commitment: Regular club meetings plus service projects.
opportunities.source_notes: Join through a specific Interact club at your school or locality.

opportunities.title: Key Club International
opportunities.short_summary: Student-led high school service organization focused on community volunteering and leadership.

opportunities.time_commitment: Year-round meetings and projects.
opportunities.source_notes: Locate a club through your school activities office or Key Club resources.

opportunities.title: North Carolina Science Festival Event Volunteers
opportunities.short_summary: Spring festival volunteer needs posted by host organizations across North Carolina, including Triangle events.

opportunities.title: NAMIWalks Wake County

opportunities.title: NCSSM Summer Accelerator
opportunities.short_summary: STEM courses for rising seventh through twelfth graders online, residential in Durham or Morganton, or day options in Hickory; tuition varies by course.
opportunities.full_description: Summer Accelerator offers engaging STEM courses taught by scientists, engineers, and university faculty. Programs include Accelerator tracks for rising 7th-9th and 10th-12th graders with residential, online, and day formats. Applications are reviewed until courses fill. A sponsored tuition-free option exists for qualifying students in Dogwood Health Trust counties in western North Carolina.
opportunities.eligibility: Rising 7th through 12th graders depending on course track; see NCSSM admissions FAQs.
opportunities.cost_text: Tuition approximately $495-$1,925 depending on course and format (per NCSSM).
opportunities.time_commitment: Summer sessions; length varies by course.
opportunities.capacity_note: Each course caps enrollment and may fill before the summer; Dogwood Health Trust funds 60 tuition-free seats annually for qualifying students in specified western NC counties per NCSSM.
opportunities.source_notes: Apply early; courses close when full. Financial aid questions are addressed in Accelerator admissions FAQs.

opportunities.title: NCSSM Early Accelerator
opportunities.short_summary: Weeklong non-residential STEM day camps for rising fifth and sixth graders on NCSSM campuses in Durham and Morganton.
opportunities.full_description: Early Accelerator offers hands-on STEM activities for younger middle school students. Programs run as day camps; students bring lunch and materials are provided. opportunities.eligibility: Rising fifth and sixth graders with interest in STEM and appropriate conduct; custodial parent completes the application.
opportunities.cost_text: $350 per program (per NCSSM Early Accelerator page).
opportunities.time_commitment: One week per course; daytime schedule.
opportunities.capacity_note: Day-camp seats per course are limited; applications accepted until filled.
opportunities.source_notes: Designed for rising fifth and sixth graders; share with younger siblings or mentors supporting middle grades.

opportunities.title: NC Teamship Showcase (NCSSM partnership)
opportunities.short_summary: Statewide Teamship experience culminating with collaboration on a real business problem; virtual start then residential week at NCSSM-Durham for selected NC students.
opportunities.full_description: Students who have participated in Teamship at a participating school or organization may apply or be nominated to join teams from across North Carolina. The program includes solving a STEM-related problem for a business partner, professional networking, written coach feedback, and a residential portion on the Durham campus with meals and housing at no cost. Optional transportation stipends may be requested. NCSSM describes 2026 application details posting in February with a tentative on-campus week in late June.
opportunities.eligibility: North Carolina students who have participated in a Teamship Experience; apply via College Board Teamship or coach nomination when the window opens.
opportunities.time_commitment: Multi-week summer experience including an on-campus residential portion; exact 2026 dates on Teamship and NCSSM pages when posted.
opportunities.capacity_note: Small statewide cohort drawn from students who already completed a Teamship experience; residential week at NCSSM-Durham.
opportunities.source_notes: NCSSM states 2026 application details should post in February on the College Board Teamship Showcase page. Program requires prior Teamship participation.

opportunities.title: Wake Forest University Online Immersion (Pre-College)
opportunities.short_summary: Self-paced online courses for ages 13+ in medicine, law, psychology, business, bioscience, and related fields with Wake Forest faculty-led content.
opportunities.full_description: The Online Immersion Program offers certificate-granting courses year-round. Topics listed on the official site include medicine, law, psychology, business strategy and innovation, infectious disease, cancer, sports medicine, women's medicine, and finance. Each course includes multimedia instruction, mentor support, and a capstone project. Need-based scholarships may be requested after applying.
opportunities.eligibility: Students ages 13 and older; no transcripts or letters of recommendation required to apply per official instructions.
opportunities.cost_text: $1,595 per course (per official program page).
opportunities.time_commitment: About 20-30 hours per course; start dates vary.
opportunities.source_notes: On-campus Summer Immersion in Winston-Salem is linked from the same site for students preferring residential options.

opportunities.title: Red Hat High School Intern (Raleigh)
opportunities.short_summary: Four-week themed team internship in Raleigh for rising juniors and seniors who live in the Raleigh or Boston areas and can work 35 hours per week on-site.
opportunities.full_description: Red Hat's High School Internship Program places students on a team-based project tied to real business priorities. The Raleigh posting lists program dates July 6-July 31, 2026, full attendance requirements, supplemental application questions, a minimum 2.5 GPA, good standing at school, and reliable transportation to the Red Hat office. Applicants must be at least 16 and a rising junior or senior for 2026-2027.
opportunities.eligibility: At least 16 years old; reside in the Raleigh or Boston area per posting; rising junior or senior for 2026-2027; meet GPA and conduct requirements.
opportunities.time_commitment: Four consecutive weeks, about 35 hours per week.
opportunities.capacity_note: Structured high school internship cohort; competitive selection and firm summer dates per posting.
opportunities.source_notes: Structured data on the posting listed datePosted 2026-03-30 and validThrough 2026-04-01; compensation is not stated in the summary text, so confirm on Workday before applying.

opportunities.title: Mathnasium of Cary: Instructor and Center Roles
opportunities.short_summary: Paid math instructor and leadership-track roles at the Cary center; apply through the center's official interest form.
opportunities.full_description: Mathnasium of Cary hires instructors to teach math using the Mathnasium Method and offers paths toward lead instructor and management roles. opportunities.eligibility: Varies by role; many centers hire capable high school and college students for instructor positions. Confirm details with Mathnasium of Cary.
opportunities.compensation_text: Wage discussed during hiring; not listed on the public careers page.
opportunities.time_commitment: Part-time shifts; schedule set with management.

opportunities.eligibility: Varies by employer; students must verify each posting independently.
opportunities.time_commitment: Varies by role.
opportunities.source_notes: Aggregator search only. Prefer applying through an employer's official careers page when you can identify the hiring organization.

opportunities.title: Wake Forest Summer Immersion (On-Campus)
opportunities.short_summary: Residential summer courses on the Winston-Salem campus for high school students exploring subjects with Wake Forest faculty.
opportunities.full_description: Wake Forest University offers Summer Immersion courses on campus so students can study with professors outside an online-only format. opportunities.eligibility: High school students per Summer Immersion admissions; see the on-campus site for grade or age rules for each institute.
opportunities.cost_text: Program tuition and fees vary by institute; see official Summer Immersion pages.
opportunities.time_commitment: Multi-day summer institutes; schedules vary.
opportunities.source_notes: Separate from Online Immersion; confirm current year application status, deadlines, and costs on immersion.summer.wfu.edu.

opportunities.title: Coca-Cola Scholars Program
opportunities.short_summary: National $20,000 achievement-based scholarship for graduating high school seniors who demonstrate leadership, service, and impact.
opportunities.full_description: The Coca-Cola Scholars Program recognizes students in their final year of high school for leadership, service, and commitment to school and community. Each year 150 scholars are selected to receive a $20,000 scholarship. The process includes an initial online application phase followed by semifinalist and regional finalist steps with essays, transcripts, and interviews for advancing students.
opportunities.eligibility: U.S. high school students graduating the award cycle academic year with plans for accredited post-secondary study; citizenship/residency rules on the official site; minimum GPA and eligibility exclusions (including Coca-Cola employment ties) apply.
opportunities.compensation_text: $20,000 scholarship for recipients (per foundation materials).
opportunities.time_commitment: Phase 1 application window is typically early fall each year; see official dates.
opportunities.capacity_note: 150 scholars are selected nationwide each year (per Coca-Cola Scholars Foundation).
opportunities.source_notes: National scholarship (curated). Wake Pathways prioritizes Wake, Triangle, and NC awards first. The 2027 application (for students graduating in 2026-2027) is listed to open August 1, 2026 on the foundation site; confirm each cycle’s dates on the official apply page.

opportunities.title: Jack Kent Cooke College Scholarship Program
opportunities.short_summary: Highly competitive last-dollar undergraduate scholarship for high-achieving high school seniors with financial need attending top colleges.
opportunities.full_description: The Cooke College Scholarship Program supports high-achieving high school seniors with financial need who plan to graduate from a four-year college. Awards are individualized last-dollar funding after other aid and can be substantial depending on college cost. The program includes advising on college choice, financial aid, and the transition to campus.
opportunities.eligibility: Senior standing with strong academics and demonstrated financial need; planning to enroll full-time at an accredited four-year institution; full criteria and exclusions on jkcf.org.
opportunities.compensation_text: Award size varies by student; up to about $55,000 per year is cited as a maximum on the foundation page.
opportunities.time_commitment: Annual application window; see foundation site for reopening dates.
opportunities.capacity_note: Highly competitive national selection; only a small share of applicants receive awards.
opportunities.source_notes: National scholarship (curated). Wake Pathways prioritizes Wake, Triangle, and NC awards first. As of verification the foundation stated the College Scholarship Program application was closed; monitor jkcf.org for the next intake.

opportunities.title: Golden LEAF Scholarships
opportunities.short_summary: North Carolina scholarship programs for students from rural and economically distressed counties, administered by the Golden LEAF Foundation.
opportunities.full_description: Golden LEAF Foundation scholarships support North Carolina students, often from rural and economically distressed counties, who pursue post-secondary credentials at NC institutions. Specific award types, amounts, and county eligibility change by program year; students should read the current scholarship page and any PDF guidelines linked from goldenleaf.org.
opportunities.eligibility: North Carolina residents meeting program-specific county, college, and financial criteria published on the Golden LEAF scholarships page.
opportunities.compensation_text: Award amounts vary by program; see current Golden LEAF scholarship materials.
opportunities.time_commitment: Deadlines vary by scholarship cycle; follow the official page each year.
opportunities.capacity_note: Awards are limited by annual funding; some programs target specific counties or institutions.
opportunities.source_notes: North Carolina-focused scholarship programs (priority tier for this directory). Confirm open programs, deadlines, and county lists on goldenleaf.org before applying.

opportunities.title: Amazon Future Engineer Scholarship and Internship
opportunities.short_summary: Need-based computer science scholarship for U.S. high school seniors, with renewable aid and a pathway to a summer internship at Amazon after beginning college.
opportunities.full_description: Amazon Future Engineer, with Scholarship America, offers renewable scholarship support (up to $10,000 per year for unmet need, capped per official FAQ) for seniors planning computer science-related majors who meet coursework, GPA, work authorization, and financial need requirements. Recipients who remain in good standing may also receive an offer to complete a paid summer software engineering internship at Amazon after starting college. Applications were listed as closed on the public page at verification time.
opportunities.eligibility: U.S. high school senior planning a qualifying CS or related major at an accredited four-year or 2+2 path; computer science course or Amazon assessment; minimum 2.3 GPA; demonstrated financial need; work authorization as described on amazonfutureengineer.com and Scholarship America.
opportunities.compensation_text: Renewable scholarship up to $10,000 per year toward unmet need (per program FAQ); separate paid internship after college enrollment for selected scholars.
opportunities.time_commitment: Scholarship applies across undergraduate years per renewal rules; internship occurs after starting college.
opportunities.capacity_note: A defined national cohort is selected each year; internship offers apply to scholarship recipients who continue to meet program rules.
opportunities.source_notes: National scholarship (curated). Wake Pathways prioritizes Wake, Triangle, and NC awards first. Apply through Scholarship America’s Amazon Future Engineer portal when the cycle is open; page indicated applications were closed at verification.

opportunities.title: NC State Computer Science High School Internship (CEREAL Lab)
opportunities.short_summary: Four-week, in-person summer computing internship on NC State’s campus: advanced block-based programming, generative AI, curriculum development for middle school science, and K-12 summer camp support.
opportunities.full_description: The CEREAL Lab’s Computer Science High School Internship Program runs on campus in Raleigh. For summer 2026 the official page lists June 15 through July 10, describes the opportunity as in-person and paid, and states that applications will open around April 2026 with an email alert list at go.ncsu.edu/internship-alert. Interns participate in a computing boot camp, help middle school science teachers integrate computing, build computing-infused curriculum (games, simulations, tutorials), collaborate with teachers on classroom activities, assist with K-12 summer camp learning activities, and may earn school internship credit per program materials. From 2025 through 2027, interns selected for the NSF TRACES project are eligible for a $1,500 stipend per the same page. Not all participants may be on that funded track, so confirm compensation when you apply.
opportunities.eligibility: High school students comfortable with block-based programming who have completed at least one computing course or equivalent camp/outreach; able to work in teams and independently; meets any additional requirements in the application when it opens.
opportunities.compensation_text: Official page labels the program a paid opportunity; TRACES-selected interns (2025-2027) are eligible for a $1,500 stipend per program text. Verify your cohort on the application.
opportunities.time_commitment: Four consecutive weeks full time; summer 2026 dates listed as June 15-July 10 on the official page.
opportunities.capacity_note: Competitive university-hosted internship; application includes personal statement, references, and materials described on the official page.
opportunities.source_notes: Short URL resolves to the CEREAL Lab program page. Applications for 2026 were not yet open at verification (page: open around April); sign up for alerts at https://go.ncsu.edu/internship-alert .

opportunities.title: NC State High School Summer Research Program (Natural Resources)
opportunities.short_summary: Two-week, daytime summer research experience on or near NC State’s campus exploring forestry and natural resources careers, with lunch provided and an application through NC State’s short link.
opportunities.full_description: Supported by the Weyerhaeuser Giving Fund, this program is run by the NC State College of Natural Resources with partner messaging tied to NC A&T. The official page describes seminars, mentorship, connection to undergraduate research events, and building community with peers interested in natural resource careers. Summer 2026 is scheduled July 13-24, 9 a.m. to 2 p.m. daily, on or near campus, with lunch provided. Past cohort highlights on the site referenced about 12 students in a longer historical format; current cohort size is not spelled out for 2026, so assume competitive selection.
opportunities.eligibility: High school students interested in forestry and natural resources careers; confirm any grade or residency rules in the official application.
opportunities.compensation_text: Lunch provided daily per official page; no wage or stipend is described there.
opportunities.source_name: NC State College of Natural Resources
opportunities.time_commitment: Weekdays 9 a.m.-2 p.m. for two weeks (July 13-24, 2026 per official page).
opportunities.capacity_note: Past program copy referenced a small student cohort; treat as limited and competitive unless the application states otherwise.
opportunities.source_notes: Apply link redirects to a Google Form from go.ncsu.edu/hssummerapp. Questions: zakiya_leggett@ncsu.edu per department page.

opportunities.title: Wake County EMS High School Summer Internship
opportunities.short_summary: Structured summer EMS internship for selected Wake County high school juniors and seniors: academy, simulations, field trips, specialty classes, and ambulance observation, totaling 120 hours aligned with WCPSS internship standards.
opportunities.full_description: Wake County Government’s EMS Programs for Youth page describes a competitive high school summer internship for juniors and seniors. Activities include time in the EMS academy, simulations, field trips, specialty classes, ambulance observation shifts, exposure to EMS system departments, and a final presentation. Successful completion yields 120 hours that may count toward one credit hour at participating high schools per the official page. Program location is the Wake County Emergency Services Education Center (221 South Rogers Ln., Suite 160, Raleigh). Calendar combines weekdays and ambulance shifts; students must provide their own transportation. Important: The public page text still references a prior cycle (for example June-August 2025 and a March 1, 2025 application deadline). Re-read the live Wake County EMS page for the current year’s dates, eligibility, and the active application link before applying.
opportunities.eligibility: High school junior or senior; able to complete the full 120-hour program including final presentation; parental or guardian approval; reliable transportation on and off site. Preference described for students interested in EMS careers and/or prior Wake County EMS youth program participation.
opportunities.time_commitment: 120 hours over summer months; weekdays plus ambulance shifts per official page (confirm schedule for the current cycle).
opportunities.capacity_note: Selected students only; competitive application with essays and interview noted on the official page.
opportunities.source_notes: Embedded Microsoft Forms application URLs on the county page may rotate by cycle; rely on the live EMS Programs for Youth page for the authoritative deadline and form. Page last updated metadata showed Feb 12, 2026 while body text still cited 2025 dates at verification.

opportunities.title: Achievement Scholarship for Refugees and Immigrants of Wake County
opportunities.short_summary: Up to $16,000 over four years for Wake County high school seniors who are immigrants, refugees, or first-generation U.S. citizens attending college in North Carolina.
opportunities.full_description: Endowed through Triangle Community Foundation, this scholarship supports Wake County seniors who plan to attend an accredited public, private, or technical college or university in North Carolina full time. Awards renew for up to four years with a 2.5 GPA and semester reporting through the foundation’s universal scholarship application.
opportunities.eligibility: Wake County resident; high school senior; minimum 2.5 GPA; immigrant, refugee, or first-generation U.S. citizen; plan to enroll full time at an accredited NC postsecondary institution.
opportunities.compensation_text: Up to $16,000 total (paid annually up to four years per award page).
opportunities.time_commitment: Annual Triangle CF universal application; confirm each cycle’s deadline on the award page.
opportunities.source_notes: Uses Triangle Community Foundation’s universal online application (Grant Interface). The live page listed March 9, 2026 as the deadline for the prior cycle at verification; confirm the next year’s date before applying.

opportunities.title: Maude P. McInnes Extension & Community Educational Fund Scholarship
opportunities.short_summary: $1,000 scholarship for Wake County high school seniors attending a North Carolina college or technical school full time.
opportunities.full_description: The Wake County Extension and Community Association awards this scholarship in memory of the county’s first home economics agent. Recipients are selected based on academics, plans for full-time NC postsecondary enrollment, and materials including an acceptance letter, sealed transcript, and three references. Preference is described for family and consumer sciences interest and 4-H or Extension association ties.
opportunities.eligibility: Wake County resident; high school senior; minimum 2.5 GPA; accepted to attend a NC college, university, community, or technical school full time (12+ credits per semester).
opportunities.compensation_text: $1,000 one-time award (per Wake County Extension posting).
opportunities.source_name: NC Cooperative Extension — Wake County Center
opportunities.time_commitment: One-time award; application typically due late March annually.
opportunities.source_notes: Download the application packet from the news article linked above. The 2026 cycle deadline on the page was March 25, 2026.

opportunities.title: Sigma Rho Zeta High School Scholarship (Cary Zetas)
opportunities.short_summary: One-time $1,500 scholarship for Wake County graduating seniors with at least a 3.0 unweighted GPA.
opportunities.full_description: The Sigma Rho Zeta Chapter of Zeta Phi Beta Sorority offers a one-time award for full-time study in the next academic year. Funds are sent to the college financial aid office. Applicants need proof of enrollment before disbursement, and recommendation letters must be submitted by the chapter’s deadline through the provided process.
opportunities.eligibility: Graduating high school senior in Wake County; minimum 3.0 unweighted GPA; enrolling full time at an accredited college or university for the fall term (mid-year graduates may still apply per chapter site).
opportunities.compensation_text: $1,500 one-time award.
opportunities.source_name: Sigma Rho Zeta Chapter, Zeta Phi Beta Sorority, Inc.
opportunities.time_commitment: Application window opens December 1 each year per chapter site.
opportunities.source_notes: Electronic application and recommender links are on the chapter page. The 2026 cycle listed a February 28, 2026 deadline.

opportunities.title: KWF President’s Scholarship (Delta Sigma Theta)
opportunities.short_summary: One-time $15,000 award for an African American female senior in the Knightdale–Wake Forest chapter service-area schools.
opportunities.full_description: The Knightdale-Wake Forest Alumnae Chapter of Delta Sigma Theta Sorority awards this scholarship to a high school senior who will enter college in the upcoming fall. Applicants must attend a school in the chapter’s published service list and meet GPA requirements. Additional KWF scholarships on the same page may fit other Wake County students.
opportunities.eligibility: African American female; senior at a listed high school in the KWF service area (e.g., East Wake, Heritage, Knightdale, Millbrook, Rolesville, Wakefield, Wake Forest, and others on the official page); minimum 3.5 unweighted GPA.
opportunities.compensation_text: $15,000 one-time award (per chapter scholarship page).
opportunities.source_name: Knightdale-Wake Forest Alumnae Chapter, Delta Sigma Theta Sorority, Inc.
opportunities.time_commitment: Annual deadline in mid-February; confirm the current year URL on kwfalumnae-dst.org.
opportunities.source_notes: Online application is linked from the chapter scholarships page (Google Forms). Update to the new year’s page when posted.

opportunities.title: Garner EDC Endowed Scholarship (Garner & South Garner HS → Wake Tech)
opportunities.short_summary: Scholarships for Garner Magnet and South Garner High graduates with financial need who attend Wake Technical Community College.
opportunities.full_description: An endowed fund from the Garner Economic Development Corporation supports graduates of Garner Magnet High School and South Garner High School who enroll at Wake Tech. Awards are administered through the Wake Tech Foundation and emphasize students with at least a 2.5 GPA and demonstrated financial need.
opportunities.eligibility: Graduating senior from Garner Magnet High School or South Garner High School; planning to attend Wake Tech; minimum 2.5 GPA; financial need per foundation materials.
opportunities.compensation_text: Award amounts vary; endowment supports multiple annual awards (see Wake Tech news release).
opportunities.time_commitment: Apply through Wake Tech Foundation scholarship processes; confirm current deadlines on waketech.edu.
opportunities.source_notes: Primary announcement is a Wake Tech news story describing the Garner EDC endowment; students should use the Wake Tech Foundation for current application steps.

opportunities.title: Triangle Scholarship Fund
opportunities.short_summary: Up to $10,000 over four years for a Triangle high school senior who shows extraordinary community commitment (plus an adult learner award).
opportunities.full_description: Triangle Community Foundation selects one high school senior and one adult learner who demonstrate strong community impact. High school applicants must be residents of Durham, Orange, Wake, or Chatham counties and plan to attend a public or private college or university in North Carolina. Per the award page, the high school recipient is drawn from the applicant pool for the Benjamin F. Ward Scholarship; adult learners apply via the Joseph Nystrom Working Student Scholarship—follow those award instructions to be considered for the Triangle Scholarship Fund.
opportunities.eligibility: Minimum 2.5 GPA; resident of Durham, Orange, Wake, or Chatham County; plan to enroll at a NC college or university; meet criteria for the related Ward or Nystrom scholarship process as described on trianglecf.org.
opportunities.compensation_text: Up to $10,000 total, paid annually up to four years with GPA and reporting requirements.
opportunities.time_commitment: Part of Triangle CF’s universal application each spring.
opportunities.source_notes: Read the “How to apply” section carefully: high school students apply through the Benjamin F. Ward Scholarship listing to be considered.

opportunities.title: GSK Opportunity Scholarship
opportunities.short_summary: Up to $20,000 over four years for Chatham, Durham, Orange, or Wake County residents attending a public university in North Carolina.
opportunities.full_description: Funded by GSK and administered by Triangle Community Foundation, this renewable award supports Triangle residents who show potential to succeed despite adversity. Payments are up to $5,000 per year for up to four years for students attending North Carolina public colleges or universities, with renewal tied to academic standing and semester reports.
opportunities.eligibility: Minimum 2.5 GPA; resident of Chatham, Durham, Orange, or Wake counties; must attend or plan to attend a public college or university in North Carolina.
opportunities.compensation_text: Up to $5,000 per year for up to four years ($20,000 total maximum per award page).
opportunities.time_commitment: Triangle CF universal application; confirm annual deadline on the award page.
opportunities.source_notes: March 9, 2026 was listed as the application deadline for the prior cycle at verification.

opportunities.title: Assistance League of the Triangle — High School Senior Scholarships
opportunities.short_summary: Multiple awards for seniors in Wake, Durham, or Orange counties enrolling at nonprofit NC colleges or technical schools.
opportunities.full_description: Assistance League of the Triangle Area offers scholarships for graduating seniors who live in Wake, Durham, or Orange counties and will attend nonprofit postsecondary or technical institutions in North Carolina. Criteria include academic performance, financial need analysis, and citizenship or permanent resident status per published guidelines.
opportunities.eligibility: U.S. citizen or permanent resident; resident of Wake, Durham, or Orange County; graduating senior; minimum 2.5 GPA; enrolling at a nonprofit NC postsecondary or technical institution.
opportunities.compensation_text: Award sizes vary by fund (chapter materials cite a wide range; see current guidelines).
opportunities.source_name: Assistance League of the Triangle Area
opportunities.time_commitment: Annual application; materials are typically due March 1.
opportunities.source_notes: Scholarship forms and details are linked from altriangle.org/scholarships/ and philanthropic programs pages; confirm the PDF for the current year.

opportunities.title: Carol Ann Beerstecher Nursing Scholarship
opportunities.short_summary: Up to $10,000 over two years for Triangle or Person County residents admitted to an undergraduate nursing program in North Carolina.
opportunities.full_description: This Triangle Community Foundation award honors Carol Ann Beerstecher’s nursing legacy. Recipients must be residents of Chatham, Durham, Orange, Wake, or Person County and admitted to an undergraduate nursing program in North Carolina. The award pays up to $5,000 per year for up to two years with renewal requirements.
opportunities.eligibility: Resident of Chatham, Durham, Orange, Wake, or Person County; admitted to or enrolled in an undergraduate nursing program in NC; minimum 2.0 GPA; financial need considered.
opportunities.compensation_text: Up to $5,000 per year for up to two years ($10,000 maximum).
opportunities.time_commitment: Triangle CF universal application.
opportunities.source_notes: Wake County seniors committed to nursing can apply once they have program admission.

opportunities.title: North Carolina Jaycees Outstanding Teenager Scholarship
opportunities.short_summary: Statewide recognition and scholarship for well-rounded NC seniors sponsored through a local Jaycee chapter.
opportunities.full_description: The program honors high school seniors (including recent graduates starting college in the award year) for scholarship, leadership, and community service. Applications are typically sponsored through a local Jaycee chapter. Triangle Community Foundation hosts the instructions and PDF application for the North Carolina Jaycee Foundation.
opportunities.eligibility: North Carolina resident; minimum 3.0 GPA; must work with a local Jaycee chapter for sponsorship if not already affiliated.
opportunities.compensation_text: Award amount varies; non-renewable per foundation description.
opportunities.source_name: North Carolina Jaycee Foundation (via Triangle Community Foundation)
opportunities.time_commitment: Deadline is typically mid-July; confirm each year on the award page and PDF.
opportunities.source_notes: Download the application PDF from the Triangle CF award page and email it as instructed. Chapter sponsorship is required.

opportunities.title: North Carolina 4-H Development Fund Scholarships
opportunities.short_summary: Statewide 4-H scholarships for graduating seniors with multiple years of membership planning NC college enrollment.
opportunities.full_description: The NC 4-H Development Fund and related endowments award scholarships to enrolled 4-H members who are graduating seniors. Programs typically require several years of 4-H participation, academic achievement, and enrollment at a North Carolina college or community college with transferable programs. CFNC and 4-H resources summarize options and deadlines.
opportunities.eligibility: North Carolina 4-H member; graduating high school senior; multi-year 4-H participation and other criteria per each fund’s description.
opportunities.compensation_text: Varies by fund (many awards in the hundreds to low thousands of dollars).
opportunities.time_commitment: Annual deadline commonly February 1; follow NC 4-H scholarship communications.
opportunities.source_notes: Wake County 4-H members should also follow guidance from their county extension office; CFNC summarizes fund names and links to NC 4-H application materials.

opportunities.title: North Carolina Teaching Fellows Program
opportunities.short_summary: Forgivable loan support up to $10,000 per year for students who commit to teaching in NC public schools after graduation.
opportunities.full_description: Teaching Fellows supports North Carolina students who pursue approved education majors at partner universities. Funding is structured as forgivable loans tied to teaching service in North Carolina public schools after graduation, including differentiated service for low-performing schools. High school seniors and current college students may apply during published windows.
opportunities.eligibility: North Carolina residency and admission to a partner institution in an eligible education program; full criteria and service obligations on Teach NC and official state FAQ pages.
opportunities.compensation_text: Up to $10,000 per year for up to four years (forgivable loan; service required).
opportunities.time_commitment: Multi-year undergraduate support with post-graduation teaching service.
opportunities.source_notes: This is a service-linked forgivable loan, not a gift aid grant. Read the official FAQ for partner schools and repayment/forgiveness rules.

opportunities.title: Goodnight Scholars Program (First-Year, NC State)
opportunities.short_summary: Up to about $24,000 per year for eight semesters for eligible in-state NC State first-years in STEM or STEM education majors.
opportunities.full_description: The Goodnight Scholars Program invites a subset of admitted NC State applicants to apply after receiving a mid-December invitation. It targets North Carolina residents graduating from NC high schools who intend to major in eligible STEM or STEM education fields and meet income eligibility (for example, household AGI limits published on the program site).
opportunities.eligibility: North Carolina resident graduating from a NC high school; admitted to NC State in an eligible major; invitation to apply; income and academic criteria per goodnight.ncsu.edu.
opportunities.compensation_text: About $24,000 per year for four years (eight semesters) per NC State program page.
opportunities.source_name: Goodnight Scholars Program, NC State University
opportunities.time_commitment: Invitation in mid-December; application due mid-January per published timeline.
opportunities.source_notes: Students cannot apply without an invitation. Use the Special Circumstances Form on the program site if you believe you were overlooked.

opportunities.title: Park Scholarships (NC State University)
opportunities.short_summary: Four-year merit scholarship at NC State covering tuition, fees, room, board, books, enrichment, and more for selected first-year applicants.
opportunities.full_description: Park Scholars are chosen for scholarship, leadership, service, and character. The program requires a separate application aligned with NC State fall first-year admission. Benefits include full tuition and fees, room and board, books, supplies, travel, personal expenses, and summer enrichment funding as described on park.ncsu.edu.
opportunities.eligibility: First-year fall applicant to NC State; minimum 3.80 unweighted GPA; U.S. citizen, permanent resident, or graduate of a U.S. high school per FAQ; not open to transfer applicants.
opportunities.compensation_text: Comprehensive four-year award covering tuition, fees, room, board, and other listed costs.
opportunities.time_commitment: Follow Park and NC State admissions calendars each fall.
opportunities.source_notes: Test-optional policies may apply; confirm current Park and NC State admissions requirements.

opportunities.title: Morehead-Cain Scholarship (UNC-Chapel Hill)
opportunities.short_summary: Four-year full cost-of-attendance merit program at UNC-Chapel Hill with summer enrichment and Discovery Fund opportunities.
opportunities.full_description: The Morehead-Cain Scholarship is one of the most selective merit programs in the country. Scholars receive four years of funding for the full cost of attendance at UNC-Chapel Hill plus summer programs and discovery funding. North Carolina students may apply through school nomination or other published pathways; all candidates must also complete UNC admissions requirements.
opportunities.eligibility: On track to graduate high school; competitive applicant to UNC-Chapel Hill; meet Morehead-Cain nomination or direct-application criteria outlined on moreheadcain.org.
opportunities.compensation_text: Full cost of attendance for four years plus summers (per foundation materials).
opportunities.time_commitment: Application timeline posted annually; aligns with UNC first-year admission.
opportunities.source_notes: UNC-Chapel Hill is in Orange County but is a primary target school for many Wake County students.

opportunities.title: North Carolina Opportunity Scholarship (K-12 Private School)
opportunities.short_summary: State-funded tuition assistance for eligible North Carolina students in grades K-12 attending participating private schools.
opportunities.full_description: Administered by NCSEAA, the Opportunity Scholarship helps families pay tuition and required fees at participating private schools. Awards are income-based and are not usable for college expenses. NCSEAA’s main website links to the K-12 portal and publishes priority application timelines.
opportunities.eligibility: North Carolina students entering kindergarten through 12th grade at eligible private schools; household income within program tiers; other rules on NCSEAA materials.
opportunities.compensation_text: Award levels depend on income tier (see current NCSEAA tables).
opportunities.source_name: North Carolina State Education Assistance Authority
opportunities.time_commitment: Annual priority application window in early spring per NCSEAA announcements.
opportunities.source_notes: NCSEAA redirects K-12 applications to k12.ncseaa.edu; if a link fails to load, use the “Explore K12 Funding” menu on www.ncseaa.edu. This is not a college scholarship.

opportunities.title: Class 101 — 101 Scholarship
opportunities.short_summary: National merit scholarship for U.S. high school students in grades 9-12 who demonstrate leadership and community impact.
opportunities.full_description: Class 101 awards multiple $3,000 scholarships annually to students who have not previously won. Applicants submit an essay or video describing how they used their skills to create positive change. Local Class 101 franchises, including Wake County offices, promote the program, but eligibility is national.
opportunities.eligibility: U.S. high school student in grades 9-12; has not previously won the 101 Scholarship.
opportunities.compensation_text: Four $3,000 awards annually per Class 101 materials.
opportunities.time_commitment: Applications typically open in February and close in mid-March.
opportunities.source_notes: The 2026 cycle listed February 1–March 15 dates on the public page; confirm the next cycle on class101.com.

opportunities.title: Raleigh Summer Youth Employment Program (RSYEP)
opportunities.short_summary: Paid summer employment and career exploration for Raleigh residents ages 15-18 who are in high school.
opportunities.full_description: The City of Raleigh operates RSYEP for teens who live inside Raleigh city limits. Participants work across city departments and partner sites, build workplace skills, and must meet attendance and conduct expectations. WCPSS materials have referenced both in-person and virtual track options for certain career themes; read the current city posting for the exact year’s structure.
opportunities.eligibility: Age 15-18; live within Raleigh city limits; high school student as of the program’s reference date; able to attend orientation and complete screening requirements listed on the city site.
opportunities.compensation_text: Hourly paid employment (rate set by the city each year).
opportunities.time_commitment: About eight weeks in summer; full schedule on the city site.
opportunities.source_notes: Only students living inside Raleigh municipal limits qualify even if they attend Wake County schools elsewhere.

opportunities.title: Raleigh Dream Center Summer Leadership Program
opportunities.short_summary: Paid summer ministry and leadership internship for ages 16-24 serving Raleigh neighborhoods and programs.
opportunities.full_description: Interns work Monday through Friday on teams focused on kids’ camps, outreach, and community events. Compensation is hourly with different rates for standard interns and outreach leader roles as published on the Dream Center site. The program is explicitly faith-based; applicants should review expectations around ministry participation.
opportunities.eligibility: Ages 16-24; able to attend orientation and commit to the summer schedule including required Saturdays.
opportunities.compensation_text: Paid hourly (program pages cited $7.25/hr for interns and $8.25/hr for outreach leaders in 2026).
opportunities.time_commitment: Eight weeks, weekday daytime hours plus Saturday events.
opportunities.source_notes: The 2026 recruitment window listed April 1–May 1 on the program page; confirm future years directly on raleighdreamcenter.org.

opportunities.title: ISHTaR Internship (UNC School of Medicine)
opportunities.short_summary: Paid summer research and health careers internship for North Carolina high school students through UNC Emergency Medicine.
opportunities.full_description: The Internship in Science, Health Training, and Research (ISHTaR) introduces North Carolina teens to medicine, public health, and research through UNC’s Department of Emergency Medicine. Activities can include literature review, health communication projects, shadowing, and EMS exposure. The program is competitive with many applicants each year.
opportunities.eligibility: Rising sophomore, junior, or senior at a North Carolina high school; at least 16 years old by May 31 of the program year; interest in health careers.
opportunities.compensation_text: Paid stipend described in UNC program materials.
opportunities.source_name: UNC School of Medicine — Department of Emergency Medicine
opportunities.time_commitment: Summer session dates published annually on the ISHTaR site.
opportunities.source_notes: Wake County students can commute to Chapel Hill; confirm each summer’s application window on the live ISHTaR page.

opportunities.title: NCDOT High School Internship Program
opportunities.short_summary: Paid, part-time internships for North Carolina high school students 16+ across NCDOT divisions, including Raleigh-area placements.
opportunities.full_description: NCDOT hires high school students for year-round, part-time internships lasting one or more semesters. Students gain exposure to engineering, planning, communications, environmental work, and other agency functions. Placements depend on division needs and location.
opportunities.eligibility: Enrolled in a North Carolina high school; at least 16 years old when employed; minimum 2.3 GPA; able to work part-time during the school year as scheduled.
opportunities.compensation_text: Paid part-time hourly employment.
opportunities.time_commitment: Part-time during semesters; may extend across consecutive terms.
opportunities.source_notes: The public page listed May 1 as an application deadline for a recent cycle; verify the current year on ncdot.gov.

opportunities.title: Durham Bulls Explorer Post 50 (TV Broadcasting)
opportunities.short_summary: Unpaid co-ed broadcasting internship for ages 14-20 producing live Durham Bulls television coverage.
opportunities.full_description: Explorer Post 50, affiliated with Learning for Life / Exploring, trains students in camera, graphics, audio, replay, and production roles for live Durham Bulls broadcasts in partnership with Capitol Broadcasting. Members must complete application, interview, and training. The experience is unpaid but provides rare sports media exposure a short drive from Wake County.
opportunities.eligibility: Ages 14-20 who have completed eighth grade; interest in television production; able to meet attendance and conduct expectations.
opportunities.time_commitment: Evening and weekend game schedules during baseball season.
opportunities.source_notes: Membership is limited; watch for open houses or application announcements on MiLB Durham and partner stations.

opportunities.title: Youth Mentoring Collaborative — Summer Social Justice Academy
opportunities.short_summary: Three-week hybrid program with a $250 stipend exploring social justice themes with Durham in-person days.
opportunities.full_description: The Summer Social Justice Academy blends virtual learning with in-person gatherings in Durham. Topics include mental health, education access, justice system contact, and economic opportunity. Participants complete a capstone project. Preference may be given to youth already in mentoring programs.
opportunities.eligibility: Rising 9th through rising 12th graders who can attend required Durham sessions; interest in civic leadership and social justice.
opportunities.compensation_text: $250 stipend for completing the program (per YMC materials).
opportunities.time_commitment: About three intensive summer weeks plus capstone work.
opportunities.source_notes: Wake County students should plan travel to Durham for required in-person portions.

opportunities.title: Builders Bridge Navigator Program
opportunities.short_summary: Trades career pathway with training intensives and hiring connections; verify Triangle placements before applying from Wake County.
opportunities.full_description: Builders Bridge guides young people through skilled trades exploration, employer immersion, hiring expos, and follow-up coaching. While much of the organization’s marketing targets the Charlotte region, Wake County schools have referenced the program in internship resource lists, so students should confirm whether cohorts or employers serve the Triangle before applying.
opportunities.eligibility: Young adults interested in HVAC, electrical, plumbing, carpentry, masonry, landscaping, and related trades; minimum ages and enrollment rules posted by Builders Bridge.
opportunities.compensation_text: Pathway may lead to paid employment after training; stipends or unpaid phases possible.
opportunities.time_commitment: Multi-phase program including development days and a hiring expo.
opportunities.source_notes: Program geography has emphasized Charlotte; call Builders Bridge or a WCPSS career coordinator to confirm options for Wake County students.
